events.title: Boston Fourth of July Parade & Declaration Reading 2026 (Copley Square to Old State House)
events.description: Boston's free Fourth of July procession steps off from Copley Square at 9:00 a.m. and marches through downtown to the Old State House, where the Declaration of Independence is read aloud from the historic balcony. It is an all-ages civic tradition that feels especially historic during America's 250th anniversary in 2026.
events.venue: Copley Square (procession route to the Old State House)

events.title: USS Constitution July 4th Turnaround Sail 2026 in Boston Harbor (Old Ironsides 21-Gun Salute)
events.description: Watch 'Old Ironsides,' the world's oldest commissioned warship afloat, get underway from the Charlestown Navy Yard for her annual Independence Day turnaround cruise into Boston Harbor. The ship fires a 21-gun salute near Castle Island before returning to her berth, a free and unforgettable July 4th tradition for families along the waterfront.

events.title: Faneuil Hall Independence Day Oration 2026 in Boston (Free July 4th Ceremony)
events.description: Step inside historic Faneuil Hall for Boston's traditional Annual Independence Day Oration, a free July 4th ceremony of patriotic speeches and music in the 'Cradle of Liberty.' It is a meaningful, family-friendly way to mark the nation's 250th birthday in the heart of downtown Boston.

events.title: Boston Harborfest 2026: 198th Army Band Free July 4th Concert (Downtown Crossing)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July with a free midday concert by the 198th Army Band at Boston Harborfest's People's Stage in Downtown Crossing. It is a festive, all-ages way to fill the afternoon before the evening's Esplanade fireworks.
events.venue: The People's Stage at The Steps, Downtown Crossing

events.title: Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular 2026 on the Esplanade (Free July 4th Concert & Fireworks)
events.description: Boston's iconic free July 4th celebration returns to the Hatch Shell for its 52nd year, with the Boston Pops led by Keith Lockhart and a spectacular fireworks finale over the Charles River. Public access to the Oval lawn opens at noon with all-ages concessions and activities, the concert begins at 7:00 p.m., and fireworks light up the sky around 9:15 p.m.
events.venue: DCR Hatch Memorial Shell, Charles River Esplanade
